findings.title: Archive failure path still reports a successful archive
findings.detail: The spec says an `openspec archive` failure must warn and continue, but this file still contains unconditional success output: `Report: Change archived: openspec/changes/archive/<date>-<CHANGE_ID>/` and the final `Implementation approved, committed, PR created: <PR-URL>, change archived.` line. On the failure path those messages are false, which can mislead the user into thinking no manual archive is needed. Make archive reporting conditional on success and use a different final message when the flow continued after an archive warning.

findings.title: No regression coverage for the reordered approve flow
findings.detail: This change modifies non-trivial control flow: Archive must now run before `git add -A`/`git commit`, and archive failures must remain non-blocking. The diff adds no automated regression coverage or executable validation for either the success path or the archive-failure path. Add coverage that verifies the new step order and confirms that archive failures only warn while Commit -> Push -> PR still proceed.

findings.title: Archive now invalidates the later proposal reads used for commit and PR generation
findings.detail: The reordered flow runs `openspec archive` before Commit, but the later steps still depend on `FEATURE_PROPOSAL` under the original `openspec/changes/<CHANGE_ID>/...` path. After a successful archive that path has moved into `openspec/changes/archive/...`, so a literal execution can no longer read the proposal in the main success path. Cache the proposal contents and issue metadata before archiving, or update the post-archive steps to use the archived path explicitly.
